title: 10 best herbal creams for neck pain

remedy_properties: Cinnamon's Anti-Inflammatory Property: Cinnamon has potent anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck muscles and joints, alleviating pain and stiffness.
Peppermint's Analgesic Property: Peppermint oil contains menthol, a natural analgesic that cools and numbs the skin, providing relief from pain and discomfort in the neck and shoulders.

Ginger's Vasodilatory Property: Ginger has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation by dilating blood vessels, which helps relax tense muscles and relieve pressure on the neck and shoulder joints.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in Camellia sinensis cream, such as theaflavins and thearubigins, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the neck muscles. This helps to relax the muscles and improve blood flow, reducing tension and discomfort in the neck and shoulders, providing relief from neck pain and promoting relaxation and well-being. It is also known to promote skin health and reduce fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the neck area, alleviating discomfort and stiffness.

Theaflavins: These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the neck muscles and joints, leading to pain relief.
Theanine: This amino acid has a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation, making it easier to manage neck pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Camellia sinensis leaves are commonly used due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and relax muscles.
Roots: Camellia sinensis roots are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the neck area.
Stems: Camellia sinensis stems are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, providing relief from neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest camellia sinensis leaves at dawn when dew is still present and leaves are at their most fragrant.
Steam the leaves for 10 to 15 minutes to release their essential oils and bring them to a temperature of 212f.
Combine 2 cups of the steamed camellia sinensis leaves with 1 cup of distilled water in a large bowl.
Infuse the mixture for 4 to 6 hours in a cool dark place allowing the leaves to steep fully.
Strain the mixture and combine it with 1 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax then heat until melted.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the affected areas of the neck.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of lemongrass can help numb or relieve pain caused by neck strain or stiffness, allowing for faster recovery and improved mobility.
Antispasmodic: Lemongrass's antispasmodic properties can help relax muscles in the neck and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to chronic neck pain and limited range of motion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The key component, citral, present in lemongrass oil has been found to reduce pain and inflammation in affected areas. When applied topically in a cream form, it helps to relax tense muscles and reduce stiffness in the neck, providing instant relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, its citrusy aroma has a calming effect, promoting relaxation and reducing stress associated with neck pain.
remedy_constituents: Citral: A terpene that acts as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the neck muscles.
Limonene: A terpene that has shown anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain and improve overall neck health.
Eugenol: A phenolic compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the neck and surrounding areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams due to their high content of citral and geraniol, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: They are used to make creams as they contain compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area.
Barks: They are used to make creams, but this part is less commonly used than the first two, as they also contain compounds that aid in pain relief and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning when they are at their peak oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place with good air circulation at 35-40°c for 2-3 days.
Steep 500 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in 2 liters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Mix 200 grams of the infused oil with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter and heat the mixture at 60-70°c until it's smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm cream's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to neck pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid, can help reduce swelling and inflammation associated with neck pain.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate tension and stress-related neck pain by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties. The key active compound, rosmarinic acid, reduces muscle spasms and inflammation, easing tension in the neck muscles. Additionally, Melissa officinalis has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that often contribute to neck pain. By applying Melissa officinalis creams topically, individuals can experience relief from neck pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: As a phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid helps with neck pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the muscles and joints.
Limonene: As a terpene, limonene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with neck strain and tension.
Eugenol: As an aromatic compound, eugenol has a sedative effect, which can help to relax tense muscles and promote better sleep, reducing the severity of neck pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used because of their high content of bioactive compounds like tannins, flavonoids, and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used because they contain a high concentration of citral, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that helps to reduce pain and inflammation.
Stems: Stems are used because they contain a mixture of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of melissa officinalis flowers at the peak of their fragrance and dry them for 2-3 days.
Steep 10g of dried melissa officinalis flowers in 100ml of cold-pressed oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil with the infused oil in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to a container.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the neck and joints.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to chronic neck pain.
Licorice saponins: These compounds have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective at reducing pain and discomfort in the neck and joints.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with neck pain because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease muscle tension. The cream's soothing properties calm irritated tissues, promoting relaxation and reducing stiffness in the neck. Additionally, licorice root extract's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic pain and discomfort. By applying this cream topically, individuals can experience relief from neck pain and improved range of motion. Regular use may also promote long-term pain management.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps with neck pain by reducing inflammation and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to pain and swelling.
Licoricidin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate neck pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area.

Licorisoflavan A: This flavonoid compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help with neck pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals and reducing inflammation in the muscles and joints.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the neck area.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the muscles and joints in the neck.
Barks: They have been found to contain glycosides and flavonoids that help to reduce pain and inflammation, making them a useful component in creams for neck pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and finely grind it into a powder using a mortar.
Combine 40 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt at 170°f for 10 minutes.
Add 10 grams of glycerin and 10 grams of distilled water to the cooled beeswax mixture and stir until well combined.
Gradually add the ground glycyrrhiza glabra powder to the mixture and stir for 5 minutes until it thickens.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck muscles and joints, providing relief from pain and stiffness.
Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of herbal ginger creams help to numb the pain caused by neck strain or injury, allowing for a sense of relief and relaxation.
Muscle relaxant effects: Ginger's natural muscle relaxant properties can help to calm tense muscles in the neck and shoulders, reducing spasms and knots that contribute to pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and warming properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, work to reduce muscle tension and relax the neck muscles. This leads to a decrease in pain and stiffness, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility. Additionally, ginger's analgesic properties help to block pain signals to the brain, providing quick and effective relief from neck pain. Regular use of ginger creams can also promote overall relaxation and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the neck area.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which contribute to the pain-relieving effects of Zingiber officinale creams on neck pain.
Beta-sitosterol: As a terpene, beta-sitosterol has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the neck muscles, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Zingiber officinale's rhizomes are commonly used to make creams for neck pain due to their high concentration of gingerols, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used in creams for neck pain relief, as they contain compounds that help reduce muscle spasms and inflammation.
Buds: Zingiber officinale's buds are used in creams for neck pain due to their high content of volatile oils, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh zingiber officinale roots with a fork, being careful not to damage the roots.
Wash the harvested roots with cold water, then dry them with a clean towel for 10 minutes.
Grate 5 grams of the dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine pulp using a grater for 5 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of distilled water and 20 grams of grated zingiber officinale pulp in a double boiler for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then pour the liquid into a cream base for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with neck strain and arthritis, thereby alleviating discomfort.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of frankincense can help numb the area, providing quick relief from acute or chronic neck pain, allowing individuals to move and function more comfortably.
Antioxidant: Frankincense's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to tissue damage and inflammation in the neck, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata, also known as frankincense, creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. It contains boswellic acids, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing swelling and pain in the affected area. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the skin helps to soothe and calm the muscles in the neck, providing relief from stiffness and discomfort. Regular application can also promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension, ultimately alleviating neck pain and improving overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Alpha-boswellic acid (ABA): ABA is known to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, which helps to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with neck pain.
Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): AKBA has potent anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the neck and surrounding areas.

Terpene fraction (including boswellic acids): The terpene fraction of Boswellia serrata contains boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with neck pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate neck pain.
Rhyzomes: They contain boswellic acids, which help reduce inflammation and relieve neck pain.
Barks: They contain boswellic acids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, providing relief from neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine 100g of beeswax and 50g of cocoa butter in a double boiler to melt.
Heat 100g of coconut oil and 20g of boswellia serrata resin to 60-70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Add 20g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil to the melted coconut oil mixture and stir well.
Gradually add the melted beeswax and cocoa butter mixture to the coconut oil mixture and stir until combined.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into glass jars and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Arnica's active ingredients, such as helenin and beta-asarone, have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the neck region.

Analgesic and anesthetic effects: The cream's analgesic properties, including the presence of sesquiterpenes like camphor and menthol, provide fast-acting pain relief and numbness to help reduce discomfort in the neck.

Stimulates blood flow and reduces stiffness: Arnica's vasoactive compounds stimulate blood circulation, which helps increase oxygenation and nutrient delivery to sore muscles, while also reducing stiffness and tension in the neck.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica montana, also known as mountain arnica, creams helps with neck pain because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease muscle tension in the neck and surrounding areas. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the skin allows it to target the affected tissues, promoting relaxation and alleviating discomfort. As a result, individuals experiencing neck pain due to strain, overuse, or injury may find relief with the application of Arnica montana cream. Regular use can also help prevent future episodes.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain associated with neck strain.
Flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol and quercetin): These antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents may help reduce inflammation, relax muscle spasms, and alleviate pain in the neck area.

Thiophene derivatives (e.g., arnicin and arnidiol): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which may help reduce pain and discomfort associated with neck injuries or muscle strain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Arnica montana are the most commonly used part in creams for neck pain because they contain a high concentration of arnica oil, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used in creams for neck pain due to their ability to absorb and retain moisture, allowing for a sustained release of arnica's active compounds.

Stems: The stems of Arnica montana are used in creams for neck pain as they contain a mixture of essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh arnica montana flowers in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers at 105°f for 2 hours to preserve their potency and color.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried arnica montana flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter to create a smooth consistency.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and forms a creamy texture that is ready to use immediately.

remedy_properties: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with neck strain or sprain.
Curcuminoids: These bioactive compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate chronic neck pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Tumerones: Tumerone and its derivatives have been shown to possess analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in relieving muscle tension and pain in the neck region.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Helichrysum italicum, also known as curry plant, creams helps with neck pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that target the root cause of neck pain. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area helps to alleviate discomfort and stiffness. Additionally, its analgesic properties work to numb the pain, providing relief from chronic or acute neck pain. This natural remedy offers a non-invasive and effective solution for managing neck pain.
remedy_constituents: N-Butylidenephthalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neck strain.
Nobilinin: A sesquiterpene lactone, nobilinin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can contribute to pain relief and tissue protection in the neck region.

Italidione: A sesquiterpene lactone, italidione has shown anti-inflammatory and analgesic activities, which can help alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain signaling pathways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the neck area.
Flowers: They contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax muscles and reduce pain in the neck.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the neck area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of helichrysum italicum flowers and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried helichrysum italicum flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of the ground powder with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax melts completely.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool before use.

remedy_properties: Relaxation-inducing compounds: Valerian root contains flavonoids, terpenes, and sesquiterpenes that promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension, alleviating neck pain caused by stress and anxiety.

Pain-relieving properties: Valerian's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the neck muscles, joints, and surrounding tissues, providing natural relief from chronic pain.
Anti-spasmodic activity: The antispasmodic compounds in valerian root can help to relax muscle spasms and cramps that contribute to neck pain, restoring normal muscle function and reducing stiffness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in valerian root, such as valerenic acid and valepotriates, work to reduce inflammation and soothe muscle tension in the neck and shoulder region. By applying valerian cream topically, individuals can experience relief from chronic or acute neck pain, improving range of motion and overall comfort. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to a self-care routine for neck pain management.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling and pain associated with neck strain.
Valerenal: A sedative constituent, valerenal may help alleviate muscle tension and spasms in the neck, promoting relaxation and pain relief.
Isovalerenol: This sesquiterpene has a sedative effect and may help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common contributors to muscle tension and neck pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: They also contain valepotriates, which have sedative and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate neck pain.
Roots: Additionally, the roots of Valeriana officinalis contain a compound called isovaleric acid, which has a soothing effect on muscles and can provide relief from neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil with 20g of dried valeriana officinalis roots for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Add 10g of beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler to 160°f.
Stir in 10ml of vitamin e oil and 10ml of lavender essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate neck pain caused by inflammation or injury.
Pain-relieving: Curcumin has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, which means it helps reduce pain perception and can provide relief from neck pain.
Anti-oxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic pain conditions like neck pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with neck pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that alleviate muscle and joint discomfort. The active compound, curcumin, inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing swelling and pain in the neck and shoulder areas. Additionally, turmeric creams promote relaxation and soothe tense muscles, allowing for improved flexibility and range of motion, making it an effective treatment for neck pain relief. It can be used as a topical application to provide quick and targeted pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, help with neck pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain pathways, making it an effective anti-inflammatory and analgesic agent.

Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid, a phenolic compound, helps with neck pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress, which contributes to the development of pain and inflammation.
Tumerone: Tumerone, a sesquiterpene, helps with neck pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain transmission, making it an effective anti-inflammatory and analgesic agent.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa due to their high curcumin content, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are another primary source of curcumin, making them a popular choice for creams due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: Leaves of Curcuma longa are used in some creams due to their antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the neck area.
remedy_recipe: Extract 2 teaspoons of dried curcuma longa root in 1 cup of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids after letting it steep for 5 minutes.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add the curcuma longa infusion and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the mixture and stir well.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before pouring into containers.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the neck region.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in purple coneflower cream help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to neck pain and stiffness by reducing oxidative stress.
Pain-relieving flavonoids: Purple coneflower is rich in flavonoids like quercetin, which have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relieve pain and discomfort in the neck.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with neck pain.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers according to local regulations and guidelines.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Combine 200 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers with 400 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 100 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the echinacea petals have infused and the beeswax has melted completely.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into clean glass jars, discard solids, and allow to cool before labeling and storing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with neck pain because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active ingredients, including Echinacea pallida, work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the neck muscles and joints, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced stiffness. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it provides a gentle, soothing warmth that relaxes tense muscles and promotes relaxation, helping to alleviate tension headaches and chronic neck pain.

St john's wort creams helps with neck pain because they contain a natural ingredient that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area. Additionally, st john's wort has been shown to have analgesic properties, making it an effective remedy for relieving mild to moderate pain.
remedy_properties: Antinociceptive activity: St John's Wort contains hyperforin, which helps to reduce pain perception by inhibiting the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from neck pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The active compounds in St John's Wort, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints of the neck, reducing pain and stiffness.

Muscle relaxant properties: Hyperforin and other compounds in St John's Wort also exhibit muscle-relaxing effects, which can help to ease muscle tension and spasms in the neck, promoting relaxation and reducing pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like hyperforin, which has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing pain and stiffness associated with neck pain.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate neck pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers by cutting fresh material at dawn or dusk.
Dry the harvested hypericum perforatum material using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 6 hours.
Grind 100 grams of dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 20 grams of ground hypericum perforatum with 80 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl.
Stir the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency and has cooled slightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with neck pain because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active ingredient, calendula oil, penetrates deep into the skin to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated muscles and joints. Additionally, its warming sensation helps relax tense muscles, easing stiffness and discomfort in the neck and shoulders. By targeting both pain and inflammation, pot marigold creams provide effective relief from neck pain, allowing for a more comfortable and active lifestyle.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by neck strain or sprain, allowing for faster healing and relief from pain.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams help protect the skin and underlying tissues from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to chronic pain and inflammation in the neck region.

Wound-healing and tissue regeneration: Pot marigold's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, making it an effective ingredient for treating wounds and skin irritations caused by neck injuries or conditions like fibromyalgia.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties in roots help reduce swelling and pain in the neck.
Flowers: Antioxidant-rich petals soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry calendula flowers, collecting at least one cup for the recipe.
Combine dried calendula flowers with carrier oils such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a 1:3 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to infuse the calendula's medicinal properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add beeswax to solidify the cream.
Whip the cream with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy, allowing it to cool before transferring.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with neck pain because of its unique ability to target the underlying causes of discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties in sage cream reduce swelling and ease tension in the muscles, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced stiffness. Additionally, sage's antioxidant and analgesic qualities help to soothe irritated nerves, promoting relaxation and reducing pain signals sent to the brain. As a result, sage creams provide effective relief from neck pain, making it an excellent addition to a self-care routine.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Herbal sage creams can help relieve muscle spasms and stiffness in the neck, reducing tension and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and thujone, reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected area, promoting healing and relaxation.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to neck pain and stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and relax tense muscles in the neck.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of sage is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making it effective for reducing pain and stiffness in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 200g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 400ml of olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 4 hours, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and combine the infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 20ml of coconut oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler at low heat for 10 minutes, then pour it into sterilized jars and let it cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme creams helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as thymol and borneol, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on the muscles and joints in the neck. This allows for increased mobility and flexibility, providing relief from tension headaches and stiffness. Additionally, creeping thyme creams' warming sensation helps to relax the neck muscles, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with neck strain, allowing for increased mobility and relief.
Pain-relieving: The menthol and thymol compounds in thyme cream have analgesic effects, which can numb the affected area and provide rapid relief from neck pain.
Muscle relaxant: Thyme's ability to relax muscles can help reduce tension and spasm in the neck region, allowing for improved posture and reduced strain on the surrounding muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots of creeping thyme contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving neck pain.
Leaves: Leaves of creeping thyme are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them suitable for topical application on the neck area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus serpyllum leaves and dry them using air circulation for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine dried thymus serpyllum leaves with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:5 by weight and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to separate solids from liquids.
Mix 10% beeswax with 90% coconut oil in a double boiler until melted and homogenized, then cool to around 120°f.
Combine the herbal oil extract with the beeswax-coconut oil mixture and whip vigorously until smooth and creamy, taking approximately 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with neck pain because of its calming and relaxing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help reduce muscle spasms and inflammation in the neck area. Additionally, the cream's gentle warmth and soothing texture can ease tension and promote relaxation, allowing for a reduction in neck stiffness and pain. By providing a sense of calm and reducing muscle strain, English lavender creams offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating neck pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds: These properties help to reduce muscle spasms, inflammation, and swelling in the neck, alleviating pain and discomfort.

Sedative and calming effects: The sedative compounds in lavender cream can promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and help to quiet the mind and body, which can contribute to reduced neck tension and pain.
Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: Lavender's pain-relieving compounds can help to numb or dull pain perception in the neck, providing quick and effective relief from discomfort and stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, lavender roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe neck pain.
Flowers: The fragrant flowers of English lavender contain linalool and linalyl acetate, which provide a calming and relaxing effect to relieve neck tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered lavender with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for an additional 20 minutes to allow the oil to infuse with the lavender properties.
Strain the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and retaining 50ml of the infused oil.
Mix the 50ml of infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat until the mixture reaches 160°f for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with neck pain because of its cooling and warming properties, which work together to relax tense muscles and reduce inflammation in the neck and shoulder area. The menthol in peppermint cream acts as a natural analgesic, providing instant relief from aches and pains. Additionally, the cream's soothing effects help to calm the nervous system, reducing muscle spasms and stiffness that often accompany neck pain. As a result, herbal peppermint creams can provide lasting relief for individuals suffering from chronic or acute neck pain.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects: Peppermint's menthol content helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the neck, providing relief from stiffness and soreness.
Muscle relaxant properties: The calcium and magnesium ions present in peppermint cream work together to relax tense muscles in the neck, reducing spasms and tension that can contribute to pain.

Cooling and warming sensation: The menthol in peppermint cream creates a cooling sensation on the skin, which can help reduce pain and discomfort by increasing blood flow to the affected area, while also providing a calming warmth to soothe sore muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain high amounts of menthol, which helps to relieve tension and ease muscle spasms in the neck.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes (underground stems) of peppermint contain a compound called borneol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves of mentha x piperita in the morning and rinse them thoroughly with clean water.
Combine 200g of dried peppermint leaves with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a large glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 4 hours or overnight in a cool dark place, shaking occasionally to facilitate infusion.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing gently to extract maximum essential oils and discard solids.
Blend the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and heat it until melted, then pour into sterile tubes or containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with neck pain because of its unique combination of soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The creams contain extracts from the herbaceous Peonia lactiflora, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to relieve tension and discomfort. When applied topically to the affected area, the cream's potent antioxidants and analgesics work together to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation, providing quick relief from neck pain, stiffness, and limited mobility. This natural remedy can be a gentle yet effective way to alleviate daily aches and pains without relying on harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal Chinese Peony Creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with neck strain, making it an effective treatment for relieving discomfort.

Muscle relaxant: The cream's herbal extracts, such as licorice root and peony root, possess muscle-relaxing properties that soothe tight muscles in the neck and shoulder region, reducing stiffness and tension.

Wound-healing and regenerative: Some Chinese Peony Creams contain herbs like ginseng, which are known for their ability to promote blood flow, accelerate wound healing, and regenerate tissue damaged by injury or strain, helping to reduce scarring and promote optimal recovery from neck pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are good for making medicinal creams for neck pain because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease stiffness in the muscles.
Roots: Roots are used to make creams for neck pain due to their rich content of glycosides, which have analgesic and antispasmodic properties that alleviate muscle cramps and tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality paeonia lactiflora roots in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Grind dried paeonia lactiflora root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, resulting in 20 grams of powdered material.
Combine the powdered paeonia lactiflora root with 100 grams of coconut oil, 50 grams of beeswax, and 25 grams of shea butter in a double boiler, heating until melted.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of aloe vera gel to the cooled mixture, blending thoroughly for 30 seconds to distribute evenly.
Pour the prepared cream into sterilized jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature before storing in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with neck pain because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area. The cream's natural ingredients, such as dandelion extract and arnica oil, work together to relax tense muscles and increase blood flow, promoting healing and reducing stiffness in the neck. This relief from inflammation and tension allows individuals to enjoy improved mobility and flexibility, alleviating the nagging ache associated with chronic neck pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, providing relief from neck pain caused by conditions such as arthritis or whiplash.

Pain-relieving properties: The cream's analgesic properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the production of pain-causing prostaglandins, help alleviate chronic neck pain caused by musculoskeletal strain or tension headaches.

Antioxidant and anti-scarring properties: Dandelion creams' antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, protect the skin from free radical damage, promote tissue repair, and reduce scar tissue formation, which can contribute to chronic neck pain caused by repetitive strain or trauma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and discomfort associated with neck pain.
Buds: The buds of the dandelion plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and 50g of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried herbs with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 4 hours to decoct the herb and infuse the oil with its properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Mix 20% of the infused oil with 80% of beeswax and heat gently until the wax is melted and the mixture is smooth.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for neck pain are topical ointments made from natural ingredients that provide relief from neck discomfort and stiffness. They work by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving circulation, thereby alleviating pain. The best herbal creams for neck pain include Eucalyptus globulus, Zingiber officinale, Capsicum annuum, Arnica montana, Curcuma longa, Salix alba, Melaleuca alternifolia, Lavandula angustifolia, Mentha x piperita, and Gaultheria procumbens. If left untreated, neck pain can lead to chronic headaches, difficulty sleeping, and decreased productivity, ultimately impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neck pain that are treated with herbal creams are often musculoskeletal conditions such as back pain, shoulder pain, and sciatica. These herbal creams typically contain ingredients like arnica, capsaicin, and methyl salicylate that help reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and alleviate stiffness. They may also contain herbs like willow bark and ginger that provide analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to ease discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with neck pain is Arnica and Peppermint. Arnica, with its anti-inflammatory properties, reduces swelling and pain in the neck area, while Peppermint provides a cooling sensation, relaxing tense muscles and improving blood circulation. The combination of these two creams can also ease muscle spasms and stiffness, allowing for a full range of motion and providing long-lasting relief from neck pain. Regular application can lead to significant improvement in discomfort and mobility.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The menthol and eucalyptol in these creams work to relax tense muscles, reducing stiffness and discomfort in the neck. The cooling sensation also helps to numb the area, providing temporary relief from pain. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, eucalyptus globulus creams can help to alleviate neck pain, allowing for improved mobility and reduced discomfort, making it easier to perform daily activities.

Zingiber officinale creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and warming properties. The active compound, gingerol, in Zingiber officinale reduces inflammation and relaxes tense muscles, providing relief from neck pain. The creams also promote blood circulation, which helps to reduce stiffness and discomfort in the neck area. Additionally, the natural warming sensation of the cream can help to ease muscle spasms and promote relaxation, making it an effective remedy for alleviating neck pain and promoting overall well-being.

Capsicum annuum creams helps with neck pain because it contains capsaicin, a natural analgesic that blocks pain receptors in the body. When applied topically, capsaicin creates a warming sensation that increases blood flow and reduces inflammation in the affected area. This increased blood flow brings oxygen and nutrients to the muscles, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms that contribute to neck pain. Regular use of Capsicum annuum creams can provide long-lasting relief from neck pain and improve overall mobility.

Arnica montana creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in Arnica, such as sesquiterpene lactones, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in muscles and joints. When applied topically to the neck area, the cream can help to relax tense muscles, improve blood flow, and reduce stiffness. This can lead to a reduction in pain and discomfort, making it easier to move and perform daily activities. Regular use may also promote long-term relief from neck pain.

Curcuma longa creams helps with neck pain because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called curcumin, which targets the root cause of pain - inflammation. Curcumin works by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing swelling and discomfort in the neck muscles and joints. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties help to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, promoting faster recovery and reducing pain. As a result, many people find relief from neck pain and stiffness with regular use of Curcuma longa creams.

Salix alba creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The white willow bark extract in these creams contains salicin, a compound that reduces pain and swelling in the affected area. By applying Salix alba creams to the neck, individuals can experience relief from tension and strain, allowing for improved flexibility and range of motion. Regular use may also help to relax muscles and promote overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for neck pain.

Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with neck pain because its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties provide relief from muscle tension and discomfort. The cream's active ingredient, tea tree oil, has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that reduce swelling and ease pain. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, individuals can experience a reduction in pain and stiffness, promoting relaxation and improved range of motion, making it an effective remedy for alleviating neck pain.

Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The calming effects of the lavender oil in the creams can help reduce muscle tension, which is often a major contributor to neck pain. The creams can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can exacerbate neck pain. By applying the creams to the affected area, individuals can experience relief from pain and discomfort, allowing for improved mobility and reduced stiffness in the neck. This can lead to a significant improvement in overall quality of life.

Mentha x piperita creams helps with neck pain because of its cooling and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint oil acts as a natural analgesic, numbing the affected area and reducing pain perception. Additionally, the menthol and eucalyptus present in Mentha x piperita creams help relax tense muscles and improve blood circulation, which can contribute to reduced stiffness and discomfort in the neck. This natural remedy provides quick and effective relief from neck pain.

Gaultheria procumbens creams helps with neck pain because they contain the essential oil wintergreen, which is a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic. Wintergreen has been traditionally used to relieve muscle and joint pain, particularly in the neck and back. The creams work by reducing inflammation and relaxing tense muscles, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced discomfort. The analgesic properties of wintergreen also help to numb the pain, providing quick relief from neck pain and stiffness.
